card1.text: We are students, just like our audience. We don't lecture from a position of authority, but discuss as peers. We break down barriers and stage fright.
card1.title: Equal approach

card2.text: Our materials are not based on impressions. We draw on verified scientific studies and data (IPCC, Climate Facts) and translate them into human language.
card2.title: Verified information

card3.text: No monologues. We use games, workshops, and discussions. We want students to figure things out for themselves and form their own opinions.
card3.title: Interactivity

features.description: We believe it is possible to talk about climate without scaring people and using incomprehensible graphs. Our approach is based on dialogue and facts.
features.title: Why does it work?

hero.badge: ✨ Welcome to the updated Telperion website!
hero.description: We inform about enviroment and teach sustainable living to the young generation.

map.description: We take our programs to students throughout the Czech Republic. Take a look at all the places where Telperion has already been.
map.layers_tip: Use the layers icon to filter specific workshops.

modal.description: Are you interested in our workshops? Leave us a message and we will get in touch soon.
modal.title: Leave us a message

fashion.description: The fashion industry is one of the biggest polluters on our planet. In this upcoming program, we will focus on the difference between fast fashion and sustainable fashion. We will examine what clothes are made of, how they make their way into our closets, and how we as consumers can make better and more responsible decisions.
fashion.status: The program is currently in the development and testing phase.
fashion.title: Program: Slow vs. Fast Fashion

klima.author: Prepared by: Martin
klima.description: Martin is currently preparing a completely new format. Fast, punchy, and packed with information.
klima.status: You have something to look forward to soon!
klima.title: Program: Climate in a Nutshell

other.subtitle: We are constantly developing new formats and learning. Here you will find programs that we are currently preparing or launching.

podcast.description: Something big is coming! We will soon be launching our own podcast. Interviews, facts without fearmongering, and sustainability from a young person's perspective.
podcast.description_highlight: The final work is coming to an end, stay tuned.
podcast.platforms_label: You will find us soon at:

fresk.desc_p1: Experience a globally popular workshop that has already been attended by more than a million people. Using a set of cards based on IPCC reports, you will work with the instructor to create a visual map of the causes and consequences of the climate crisis.
fresk.desc_p2_before: The program is ideal for both complete beginners and climate enthusiasts who want to organize their thoughts and find motivation for action.
fresk.desc_p2_note: (We will be happy to provide further details upon request)
fresk.title: Climate Fresk for the public

scouts.desc_p1: Similar to Climate Days, we move from general knowledge to specific solutions appropriate to the age of the participants.
scouts.desc_p2_scouts: With Boy Scouts and Girl Scouts
scouts.desc_p2_scouts_text: we use the children's version of the Climate Mosaic workshop, which explains climate change from its causes to its consequences. Since we tailor the program to your needs, we have a set of themed workshops based on your interests.
scouts.desc_p2_youngest_text: we focus on sensitivity to nature and the basics of its protection.
scouts.desc_p3_after: we have an information section about programs that they can implement themselves. Depending on interest, we also offer an adult version of Mozaika or Base Camp from the TEREZA center.
scouts.desc_p3_before: We work with an already formed group. We strive to ensure that our program can be used as a basis for their regular activities as a group or club.
scouts.title: Scout climate weekend

public.subtitle: We also educate outside the classroom. We offer weekend trips for scout troops and intensive workshops for the general public.

climate_days.day1_desc: We focus on a comprehensive understanding of the crisis using the proven Climate Mosaic methodology. Students discover why climate change is urgent and how it relates to their lives. It also includes a safe space for expressing emotions.
climate_days.day2_desc: Students explore ways to get involved. They examine ways to reduce their personal carbon footprint and combat climate change at the municipal and national levels (adaptation and mitigation). Through workshops, they practice critical thinking and develop their own solutions.
climate_days.desc_p1: In this package, participants will gain a comprehensive understanding of climate change, meet experts from the Czech climate scene, and find specific ways to deal with the crisis.
climate_days.desc_p2_after: As a predominantly student-run organization, we offer students a unique perspective—we don't lecture them, but discuss things with them as equals.
climate_days.desc_p2_before: We proceed from the principle of
climate_days.desc_p3: We systematically evaluate the program using sociological analysis. We monitor how participants understood the topics and how their attitudes changed. We want students to leave knowing that their actions have meaning.
climate_days.subtitle: Our flagship two-day program
climate_days.tag_level: Secondary school (grades 5-9) and lower secondary school
climate_days.title: Climate Days

fresk.description: A simplified but highly effective version of the well-known French methodology, adapted for a younger audience. Through a playful card game, students decipher the causes and consequences of climate change.
fresk.title: Climate Fresk Junior

realizations.10: Adélka Kindergarten and Elementary School, Mašovice
realizations.5: Elementary School Osmička, Roudnice nad Labem
realizations.7: T. Stolzová Elementary School, Kostelec nad Labem

schools.realizations_more: ... and soon, hopefully, at your place too!
schools.realizations_title: Where the program has already taken place
schools.subtitle: Comprehensive, interactive, and peer-to-peer programs that help students understand the bigger picture and find their own role in protecting nature.
